textPlain: Correct title would be Austrian ministry replaces Microsoft with Atos.I wish Austria has some form of national IT development teams for national products/websites, they way Denmark or UK have, and not just outsource everything to politically connected consultancies like Atos.

Has Nextcloud gotten to a point where it truly competes with Google Docs? Because every time I looked at it, it didnt look like it had feature parity. Being able to edit documents with others is one feature I want out of any alternatives that I can self-host.

Good, but this needs to happen on a much larger scale. These are "just" 1200 employees, but throughout Europe there are hundreds of millions of people working with Microsoft services and they all need to be torn out and replaced.>As for the reasoning behind this move, it was prompted by a risk analysis that showed foreign cloud services failed to meet the ministry's privacy requirements, particularly regarding GDPR compliance and the upcoming NIS2 directive.This also shows that they did it for the wrong reasons. It really doesn't matter if Microsofts services are GDPR compliant or not.

Part of a trend, but what made me an impression is that Atos helped in the transition. The old consulting companies would be more than happy to claw back some market share from the US cloud providers.
Of course! Sell the disease and the cure.
